'''Lee Greenwood''' (born 28 September 1980) is an English professional rugby league coach who is assistant-coach of the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]] and an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s, 2000s and 2010s. He played at representative level for [[England national rugby league team|England]], and at club level for the [[Sheffield Eagles]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] (two spells, one in the [[Super League]], and one in [[RFL Championship|National League One]]), the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]], [[Huddersfield Giants]], [[Leigh Centurions]], [[Oldham RLFC]] and the [[Batley Bulldogs]], as a {{rlp|wg}} or {{rlp|ce}},<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> he has coached at club level for [[Siddal A.R.L.F.C.]], and in 2014, he was appointed coach of [[Gloucestershire All Golds]].<ref name="Coach Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref>{{cite news|title=Lee Greenwood: Ex-England wing appointed All Golds coach|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/29848964|accessdate=24 November 2017|work=BBC Sport|date=31 October 2014}}</ref>

==Background==
Greenwood was born in Siddal, [[Halifax, West Yorkshire|Halifax]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.{{citation needed|date=February 2020}}

==Playing career==
===International honours===
Greenwood won two [[cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[England national rugby league team|England]] while at London Broncos, he played on the {{rlp|wg}}, and scored two [[Try (rugby)|tries]] in the 98–4 victory over [[Russia national rugby league team|Russia]] in the [[2004 European Nations Cup]] at [[Luzhniki Stadium]], [[Moscow]] on 24 October 2004, and played on the {{rlp|wg}} in the 36–12 victory over [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] in the [[2004 European Nations Cup]] Final at [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]], [[Warrington]] on 7 November 2004.

==Coaching career==
===Bradford Bulls===
On 28 Oct 2022 he was appointed assistant-coach at [[Bradford Bulls]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Telegraph & Argus|date=22 Oct 2022|accessdate=12 Oct 2023|title=LEE GREENWOOD APPOINTED ASSISTANT COACH|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/1705/lee-greenwood-appointed-assistant-coach}}</ref>

On 12 May 2023 he ruled himself out from becoming the permanent head-coach following the sacking of head-coach [[Mark Dunning]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Telegraph & Argus|date=12 May 2023|accessdate=12 Oct 2023|title=Bradford Bulls interim unlikely to take job permanently|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23516718.bradford-bulls-interim-unlikely-take-job-permanently/}}</ref>
